briefdescription.para: Maximum number of vertical taps supported by the scaler.
Maximum number of vertical phases for polyphase filter.
Bit mask for extracting output channel selection.
Maximum bytes per pixel (for RGBX/YUVX at 10-bit)
Maximum buffer size calculation based on max dimensions and pixel format.
Hardware register offset for filter coefficient memory.
Fixed-point precision for scaling step calculation (2^16)
Bit mask for extracting lower 16 bits.
Bit mask for extracting upper 16 bits.
Bilinear interpolation.
Bicubic interpolation.
Polyphase interpolation.
Scaler type enumeration - defines the interpolation algorithm used.
6-tap filter
8-tap filter
10-tap filter
12-tap filter
Supported filter tap configurations for polyphase scaling.
RGB with alpha, 8-bit per component.
YUV with alpha, 8-bit per component.
YUYV 4:2:2, 8-bit.
RGB with alpha, 10-bit per component.
YUV with alpha, 10-bit per component.
Y/UV semi-planar 4:2:2, 8-bit.
Y/UV semi-planar 4:2:0, 8-bit.
RGB packed, 8-bit per component.
YUV packed, 8-bit per component.
Y/UV semi-planar 4:2:2, 10-bit.
Y/UV semi-planar 4:2:0, 10-bit.
Y only (grayscale), 8-bit.
Y only (grayscale), 10-bit.
BGR with alpha, 8-bit per component.
UYVY 4:2:2, 8-bit.
BGR packed, 8-bit per component.
Memory pixel format enumeration - defines supported color formats.
Fixed coefficient tables for polyphase scaling filters.

detaileddescription.para: Fixed coefficient tables for polyphase scaling filters.

This table contains a 64-phase, 6-tap FIR filter bank optimized for hardware implementations that support exactly 6 coefficients per phase. This is a compact version of the 12C format without padding zeros.
Format: [XV_MULTISCALER_MAX_V_PHASES][XV_MULTISCALER_TAPS_6] Phases: 64 Active taps: 6

briefdescription.para: Fixed coefficient table for 6-tap filter with 12 coefficients per phase.

detaileddescription.para: Fixed coefficient table for 6-tap filter with 12 coefficients per phase.

This table contains a 64-phase, 6-tap FIR filter bank for vertical and horizontal scaling. The 12-coefficient format includes zero-padding to match hardware requirements. Each row represents one of 64 interpolation phases, allowing smooth sub-pixel precision during scaling operations.
Format: [XV_MULTISCALER_MAX_V_PHASES][XV_MULTISCALER_TAPS_12] Phases: 64 Active taps: 6 (with padding zeros)

briefdescription.para: Fixed coefficient table for 8-tap filter with 8 coefficients per phase.

detaileddescription.para: Fixed coefficient table for 8-tap filter with 8 coefficients per phase.
This table contains a 64-phase, 8-tap FIR filter bank in compact format without padding zeros. Optimized for hardware that supports exactly 8 coefficients per phase.
Format: [XV_MULTISCALER_MAX_V_PHASES][XV_MULTISCALER_TAPS_8] Phases: 64 Active taps: 8

briefdescription.para: Fixed coefficient table for 8-tap filter with 12 coefficients per phase.

detaileddescription.para: Fixed coefficient table for 8-tap filter with 12 coefficients per phase.

This table contains a 64-phase, 8-tap FIR filter bank providing higher quality scaling compared to 6-tap filters. The 12-coefficient format includes zero-padding for hardware alignment. 8-tap filters offer improved frequency response and reduced aliasing artifacts.
Format: [XV_MULTISCALER_MAX_V_PHASES][XV_MULTISCALER_TAPS_12] Phases: 64 Active taps: 8 (with padding zeros)

briefdescription.para: Fixed coefficient table for 10-tap filter with 10 coefficients per phase.

detaileddescription.para: Fixed coefficient table for 10-tap filter with 10 coefficients per phase.
This table contains a 64-phase, 10-tap FIR filter bank in compact format without padding zeros. Optimized for hardware that supports exactly 10 coefficients per phase.
Format: [XV_MULTISCALER_MAX_V_PHASES][XV_MULTISCALER_TAPS_10] Phases: 64 Active taps: 10

briefdescription.para: Fixed coefficient table for 10-tap filter with 12 coefficients per phase.

detaileddescription.para: Fixed coefficient table for 10-tap filter with 12 coefficients per phase.

This table contains a 64-phase, 10-tap FIR filter bank offering superior scaling quality. The 12-coefficient format includes minimal padding. 10-tap filters provide excellent frequency response characteristics and minimal ringing artifacts, suitable for professional image processing.
Format: [XV_MULTISCALER_MAX_V_PHASES][XV_MULTISCALER_TAPS_12] Phases: 64 Active taps: 10 (with minimal padding)

briefdescription.para: Fixed coefficient table for 12-tap filter with 12 coefficients per phase.

detaileddescription.para: Fixed coefficient table for 12-tap filter with 12 coefficients per phase.

This table contains a 64-phase, 12-tap FIR filter bank providing the highest quality scaling among all supported tap configurations. With no padding required, all 12 coefficients are active. 12-tap filters deliver exceptional frequency response, minimal aliasing, and superior image quality for demanding applications.
Format: [XV_MULTISCALER_MAX_V_PHASES][XV_MULTISCALER_TAPS_12] Phases: 64 Active taps: 12

briefdescription.para: Start the multi scaler core operation.

detaileddescription.para: This function enables auto-restart mode and starts the multi scaler core. It validates that the number of outputs is configured correctly and that all output channels specified by the output bit mask are properly configured.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the core instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: Stop the multi scaler core operation.

detaileddescription.para: This function disables auto-restart mode on the multi scaler core, effectively stopping its operation after the current frame completes.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the core instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: Get the number of output channels configured.

detaileddescription.para: This function retrieves the current number of output channels that are configured for the multi scaler core.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the core instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: Set the number of output channels to be used.

detaileddescription.para: This function configures the number of output channels for the multi scaler core. The number must be between 1 and the maximum number of outputs supported by the hardware instance. The output bit mask is reset when this function is called.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the core instance to be worked on.
is the number of output channels to configure.

simplesect.para: NumOuts must be greater than 0 and less than or equal to MaxOuts.

briefdescription.para: Read the configuration of a specific channel.

detaileddescription.para: This function retrieves the current configuration settings for a specified output channel. The application must set the ChannelId field in the MS_cfg structure before calling this function. The function reads all channel parameters including dimensions, color formats, strides, and buffer addresses.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the core instance to be worked on.
is a pointer to the multi scaler config structure. The ChannelId field must be pre-filled by the application.

simplesect.para: The ChannelId in MS_cfg must be set before calling this function.

briefdescription.para: Configure a channel with the specified scaling parameters.

detaileddescription.para: This function programs all configuration parameters for a specific output channel including input/output dimensions, color formats, strides, buffer addresses, and scaling rates. It also handles optional cropping window configuration and automatically programs the appropriate filter coefficients based on the scaling ratio. Extensive validation is performed on all parameters to ensure valid configuration.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the core instance to be worked on.
is a pointer to the multi scaler config structure containing all channel configuration parameters.

simplesect.para: All configuration parameters are validated via assertions. Buffer addresses must not overlap to prevent data corruption.
